taskDescription: Arrange the anatomical layers from superficial to deep when dissecting the submandibular region.
paragraphs: Skin, platysma, and investing layer of deep cervical fascia.
Anterior belly of digastric and mylohyoid muscle — the muscular diaphragm forming the floor of the mouth.
Superficial part of the submandibular gland lying below mylohyoid in the submandibular triangle.
Deep part of the submandibular gland and Wharton's duct wrapping around the posterior border of mylohyoid into the sublingual space.
Lingual nerve and hypoglossal nerve (CN XII) running on the surface of hyoglossus muscle in the floor of the mouth.
Hyoglossus muscle — deep to which lie the lingual artery and lingual vein.
